title: IntentStandard
description: The Lignos Intent Standard — a structured definition of an AI agent's business intent, product standard, anti-pattern, and value proxy. Produced by /lignos-canvas and stored in .lignos/canvas.md.

agent.description: Name or short description of the agent

version.description: Version of this standard, e.g. '1.0.0'. Increment when the one-sentence standard changes.

createdAt.description: ISO date this standard was first defined

jtbd.description: Job to Be Done — the specific situation and outcome this agent is hired to produce

situation.description: One sentence: the specific context or trigger that causes someone to use this agent

user.description: Who is in this situation — role and context

outcome.description: What they need to be able to do after the agent runs

productStandard.description: The quality bar — what great looks and feels like, beyond correctness

summary.description: One sentence: the subjective quality bar — voice, confidence, scope

tone.description: Voice and register of the output

constraints.description: Hard rules — things the agent must always or never do

antiPattern.description: Taste drift — the specific subtle failure that signals the agent is off-standard

description.description: One sentence: the concrete, observable drift pattern — not a crash, a quality failure

signals.description: Short phrases or patterns that are dead giveaways the agent has drifted. Used as blocking assertions in eval.

valueProxy.description: The observable judgment signal — how you know value was delivered without measuring a KPI

observableSuccess.description: What you see or don't see when the agent worked — a behavior change, not a metric

judgeQuestion.description: The one-sentence question a reviewer applies to decide if an output succeeded

oneLineSentenceStandard.description: The single sentence that captures the agent's entire standard: 'This agent is hired to [JTBD outcome] and must always [standard].'
